title: The Pilots Ages
wikitext:
[]: The Pilots' Ages             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Jun 17, 2010 10:00 pm I'm sure there's probably a thread for this, but I did a quick search and couldn't find anything. I'll copy/past the relevant post from the "Negative Capability" thread on the subject (there is some more, but this is the most relevant): OMF wrote: Of note here is the fact that the show never bothers explaining why only 14 year old can pilot; it's totally uncommented on. But it's clear that any explanation that could be offered is largely superfluous anyway. The overall meaning and impact of the work would not benefit by it.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jun 18, 2010 6:54 am So you are claiming that inherently, only adolescents are capable of adequately syncing with and piloting the Evas? While it's a very interesting hypothesis, and one which makes a good deal of logical sense, I can't help but think that it leaves out the connection between the children's ages and Second Impact. Along these lines, I've always felt there was some obscure connection between the pilots ability to synch and the stated decline in birth rate following 2I.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri Jun 18, 2010 1:15 pm The decline in birth rate is probably just a bit of worldbuilding, explainable by the fact that Second Impact is above and beyond EVERYTHING that's ever happened before. There's starvation (apparently even in America), a quality that rarely encourages children. There's probably all sorts of wars going on in the already unstable places of the world. Disease would spread like wildfire, especially with climate change. Finally, there's all the people who would have survived 2I but just... stopped. The land is here, the sea is there. These are borders, firm and concrete, and they suddenly stopped applying. You'd be amazed what a little destruction can do to a min.d Jimbo's theory is actually one of the best and doesn't rely on things like the SDSS predicting things (protip: it's very unlikely the SDSS did anything except explain Adam, Lilith, the Angels, the Spears, and the mechanics of an Impact), or on some strange metaphysical deal never actually hinted at. I don't know if he's the one that convinced me of it, or if I came up with it concurrently, or got the theory from a third source, but I've very firmly believed in it.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jun 18, 2010 2:00 pm Nonsense, the SDSS is a magical prophecy book that predicted everything and the lowered birth rate is from there being NO SOULS LEFT

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jun 18, 2010 3:27 pm ^ Better insert a sarcasm tag before people start pouncing.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Fri Jun 18, 2010 4:06 pm I FIRMLY believe that the reason is because, according to the DSS they have to be born AFTER Second Impact. It probably has to do with the Chamber of Guf/Gaf/Egg being empty/no souls etc... I hope everyone knows this, cuz they say it in the series, dont feel a need to do all that research when I'm sure eveyone gets the picture. NOW, the reason 2014-2015 was chosen (essentially limiting the pilots ages to 13/14 NOT imp which one) is open for debate allllllllll day. -A common Neo-scifi thing to do is to not put it in the far far future, for relevance. Also to show the desperate human survival drive to push tecnology when faced with near extinction. Could even be an arbitrarily significant year for Anno. -Maybe it was because Anno really, really wanted 14 y/o's. This could be a number of things, ranging from secret obsession with them, to them being overall more emotionally unstable. It could also have psychological dimensions, as 13/14 are crucial ages for the next developmental push for personality development. It may also be that they are more capable of maturity starting at those ages, but still universally naive enough to make big mistakes that add to battle drama. Also note that the growth the characters go thru or dont go thru is relevant to the storytelling process and its just better to start young-but-not-too-young aka 13-15. - Maybe he wanted to leave it open for spin-off's and sequels, as 14 still leaves at least 2 more chrnoicles in their lives without going into second generation's of the characters.

[]: Komm, susser tod.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jun 18, 2010 4:07 pm Legendary wrote: The decline in birth rate is probably just a bit of worldbuilding, explainable by the fact that Second Impact is above and beyond EVERYTHING that's ever happened before.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri Jun 18, 2010 4:21 pm Eva 02 wrote: I FIRMLY believe that the reason is because, according to the DSS they have to be born AFTER Second Impact. It probably has to do with the Chamber of Guf/Gaf/Egg being empty/no souls etc... I hope everyone knows this, cuz they say it in the series, dont feel a need to do all that research when I'm sure eveyone gets the picture.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Fri Jun 18, 2010 5:08 pm ^ Throughout the series they say a lot of even conflicting things about the DSS. Sure there are blunders in the plans but it still followed the correct paths. Why else wouldnt they lable it as obsolete by the end of the series? In fact, they held it even closer. They also hinted that theres much, much more to the scrolls than Nerv knows. The whole Seele attack on Nerv was to force Gendou's cards, cuz it was prob predicted that he would try to utilize 3I for himself. Also, I never implied that Birthrate is synonymous with the Chamber, because - frankly, I see it as a m00t topic. Low birthrate always happens after a huge event, in every form of civilisation. Funny because in preperation for an anticipated calamity, copulation goes up. Its also biologically accurate that during times of extreme fight-or-flight and distress, reproduction slows anatomically, such as premature birth for stressed mothers. The connection I was making is the significance of what happened to the Chamber of Guf during Second Impact and the pilots needing to be born AFTER Second Impact because of the effect on Guf.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jun 18, 2010 5:45 pm Eva 02 wrote: ^ Throughout the series they say a lot of even conflicting things about the DSS. Sure there are blunders in the plans but it still followed the correct paths. Why else wouldnt they lable it as obsolete by the end of the series? In fact, they held it even closer. They also hinted that theres much, much more to the scrolls than Nerv knows. The whole Seele attack on Nerv was to force Gendou's cards, cuz it was prob predicted that he would try to utilize 3I for himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri Jun 18, 2010 6:35 pm Eva 02 wrote: what happened to the Chamber of Guf during Second Impact

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jun 18, 2010 8:06 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: So you are claiming that inherently, only adolescents are capable of adequately syncing with and piloting the Evas? ... While it's a very interesting hypothesis, and one which makes a good deal of logical sense, I can't help but think that it leaves out the connection between the children's ages and Second Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sat Jun 19, 2010 3:36 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Maybe the age cutoff is before simply because they would be too old, who knows. I also think this could definitely be one example of an abandoned idea from early where they meant to make something of being born after/around 2I but the major importance became the age itself and its connection to the Eva/mother/duality themes.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jun 19, 2010 4:04 pm Mere coincidence explains the main pilots but if all of class 2A are candidates there simply must be some sort of age-related purpose at work. Perhaps 2I did something to gestating fetuses that makes the synchronizing process possible?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sat Jun 19, 2010 7:31 pm Except for how none of the children had been conceived by Second Impact. Looking at the five normal ones whose ages we know (in order from oldest to youngest: Shinji, Kensuke, Asuka, Toji, Hikari), nearly all of them were born a year or more after Second Impact. Shinji, the exception, was born in July, implying he was conceived in November, October at the earliest AND assuming he was a late birth.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jun 20, 2010 2:03 am Legendary wrote: Shinji, the exception, was born in July, implying he was conceived in November, October at the earliest AND assuming he was a late birth.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Jun 20, 2010 2:51 am My bad on Shinji. Unfortunately, your claim about Hikari and Toji is wrong. Hikari is stated to be an '02 birth, Toji an '01. Not that it matters, since Asuka and (if you want to assert that Asuka's a year older than stated) Kensuke were STILL born in the wrong time frame and one WAS a pilot and the other was a candidate. If 2I had something to do with it, they wouldn't have wasted time with either.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jun 20, 2010 4:31 am Perhaps the affects of 2I continue to effect children born afterward? I know it's a complete fanwank, but there simply must be some sort of in-universe reason why all the pilots are in the same age bracket. For Shinji, Asuka and Rei this can be ignored, but Toji and class 2A as candidates simply requires some sort of explanation.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sun Jun 20, 2010 10:48 am NemZ wrote: I know it's a complete fanwank, but there simply must be some sort of in-universe reason why all the pilots are in the same age bracket. For Shinji, Asuka and Rei this can be ignored, but Toji and class 2A as candidates simply requires some sort of explanation.

[]:             Quote Post by Adamski » Sun Jun 20, 2010 2:21 pm The pilots are all adolescents so the viewer will have characters in which they could relate to.
